I kiss the mic with affection
My mind is taking time for some stretchin'
Bending galaxies into my verbal collection
My ethics remain the most pristine

Drugs, violence and money ain't my scene
That shit's obscene, failure is not in my genome
I eat rappers like amino

I must boil your blood because I can't leave evidence
I can't have a conscious that is decadence
I'm perfecting the elements, so I can raise a hell-ish genesis

Your brain is my new residence
So prepare for ideas of excellence and long nights of restlessness
I'm reckless, I'll erode your skeletal with tetanus
Spit the most venomous, leave you stranded in a nebulous
Unless you're in for my best interest
I'll show you benevolence, fly us away on a Pegasus
Let you benefit from my intelligence
My rhymes are the perception of medicine that is perfection
Known to cure cancer, bulimia and depression
That last one is a self-tested assessment

The work was pretty good
She thought we shouldn't wait for the frost
And I thought maybe we should
We was drinking more in those days
And tempers reached a pitch
And like a fool I let her run
With the rambling itch

Think I'll just lay me down
Under this old tree
On and on we go
Through this old world a-shuffling
If you've got a truffle dog
You can go truffling

remark.lineNext: From the fool's gold mouthpiece, the hollow horn
stanza: Pointed threats they bluff with scorn
Suicide remarks are torn
From the fool's gold mouthpiece, the hollow horn
Plays wasted words, proves to warn
That he not busy being born

Cry of pain

Teasing all the while
Now dancing down the drain

They gently mock
And lift him on their backs
Lay him on his rack
And leave beneath the light

rack.linePrev: And I'm a scholar in college but don't ever say that I'm frat rap
rack.line: That's whack crap about smashing fat racks and raping girls with fat asses
rack.lineNext: If I ever saw that happen, I would rip off your nutsacks!
stanza: I started out as whack, I took the time to fine tune my craft
Fast forward my time lapse
Now I rhyme tight with no slack, I'm back, I'm back, I'm back, I'm back
I'm back and you're addicted to me like smack
So Inhale my words like crack, relax and let your eyes roll back
I'll align your spine with a tap
I can see my abs now I sat up, I got a six-pack now
But that doesn't add up, my hunger should mean I'm Santa Claus fat now
At last I finally found my knack
I took the bite from the apple on your mac
And regurgitated it back into this rap
I got more lines than topography maps
I more obsessed with myself than making sure that you clap
But I know you're sitting there in your chair
Making your head move forward and back
I molded these rhymes to be harder than a cast
I read (Read) and breathe more jazz than a sax
I stick to my pacts I'm truthful like that
I snack on anything like a pack of rats
I'll give you a smack if you say you hate blacks
But as a matter of fact, I hate black cats
If I see one in my path, fuck that I'll take the long way back
And I'm a scholar in college but don't ever say that I'm frat rap
That's whack crap about smashing fat racks and raping girls with fat asses
If I ever saw that happen, I would rip off your nutsacks!
I kid, but don't give me a reason to create a bloodbath

Your love will be safe with me

resemble.line: See how they resemble one another's
resemble.lineNext: Even in their plastic little covers?"

We were looking at the bags
Wondering if any of the colors
Matched any of the names we knew on the tags
You said "See, look it, that's yours
Stacked on top with your brother's
See how they resemble one another's
Even in their plastic little covers?"

I don't know, but it sounded heavenly
Show me someone better than me
Uh, I just smoked a Backwood, I didn't even get that high
I got buzzed as fuck though, that Back-tobacco dry, haha
And I'm back in the woods rolling backwoods that are that good
I'm a slaphappy rap master, my favorite sound is the sound of laughter
I do unseen good deeds like I'm Casper
I've touched more lives than a badass pastor
I'm shutting on this beat I treat it like the crapper
I know too many lines to be a bad actor
I'm so much more than just a rapper
Listen to my whole album then tell me why after
And I'm just breaking the ice, this cypher's just the fracture

ree.line: The meadowlark and the chim-choo-ree and the sparrow
ree.lineNext: Set to the sky in a flying spree, for the sport of the pharaoh
stanza: The meadowlark and the chim-choo-ree and the sparrow
Set to the sky in a flying spree, for the sport of the pharaoh
Little while later the Pharisees dragged a comb through the meadow
Do you remember what they called up to you and me, in our window?

rote.linePrev: You taught me the names of the stars overhead, that I wrote down in my ledger—
rote.line: Though all I knew of the rote universe were those Pleiades, loosed in December
rote.lineNext: I promised you I'd set them to verse so I'd always remember
stanza: Anyhow, I sat by your side, by the water
You taught me the names of the stars overhead, that I wrote down in my ledger—
Though all I knew of the rote universe were those Pleiades, loosed in December
I promised you I'd set them to verse so I'd always remember

rangily.linePrev: The butterflies and birds collide at hot, ungodly hours
rangily.line: And my clay-colored motherlessness rangily reclines—
rangily.lineNext: Come on home, now! All my bones are dolorous with vines
stanza: And everything with wings is restless, aimless, drunk and dour;
The butterflies and birds collide at hot, ungodly hours
And my clay-colored motherlessness rangily reclines—
Come on home, now! All my bones are dolorous with vines

recoil.line: And though our bodies recoil from the grip of the soil-
stanza: I wanted to say: Why the long face?
Sparrow, perch and play songs of long face
Burro, buck and bray songs of long face!
Sing, I will swallow your sadness, and eat your cold clay
Just to lift your long face;
And though it may be madness, I will take to the grave
Your precious long face
And though our bones they may break, and our souls separate-
Why the long face?
And though our bodies recoil from the grip of the soil-
Why the long face?

riverside.linePrev: Down to the swimming hole, where there grows a bitter herb
riverside.line: That blooms but one day a year, by the riverside —
stanza: But I took my fishing pole (fearing your fever)
Down to the swimming hole, where there grows a bitter herb
That blooms but one day a year, by the riverside —
I’d bring it here:
Down by the Riverside motel
It's ten below and falling
By a ninety-nine-cent store
She closed her eyes and started swaying
But it's so hard to dance that way
When it's cold and there's no music
Oh, your old hometown's so far away
But inside your head there's a record that's playing

rattlesnake.line: We stood and watched her gape like a rattlesnake
rattlesnake.lineNext: And pant and labor over every intake
stanza: We stood and watched her gape like a rattlesnake
And pant and labor over every intake

partner.line: And pant and labor over every intake

rattlesnake.songTitle: The King of Carrot Flowers, Pt. One
rattlesnake.linePrev: And how you built a tower tumbling through the trees
rattlesnake.line: In holy rattlesnakes that fell all around your feet
rattlesnake.lineNext: And your mom would stick a fork right into daddy's shoulder
stanza: When you were young, you were the king of carrot flowers
And how you built a tower tumbling through the trees
In holy rattlesnakes that fell all around your feet
And your mom would stick a fork right into daddy's shoulder
And dad would throw the garbage all across the floor
As we would lay and learn what each other's bodies were for

rattlesnake.linePrev: Let's follow that fire truck, I think your house is burnin' down
rattlesnake.line: And go down to the hobo jungle and kill some rattlesnakes with a trowel
rattlesnake.lineNext: And we'll break all the windows in the old Anderson place
stanza: Let's follow that fire truck, I think your house is burnin' down
And go down to the hobo jungle and kill some rattlesnakes with a trowel
And we'll break all the windows in the old Anderson place
We'll steal a bunch of boysenberries and I'll smear 'em on your face
I'll get a dollar from my mama's purse and buy that skull and crossbones ring
And you can wear it round your neck on an old piece of string
Where she’d lain, as still as a stone, in my palm, for a lifetime or two;
Then saw the treetops, cocked her head, and up and flew
(While back in the world that moves, often, according to
The hoarding of these clues)
Dogs still run roughly around
Little tufts of finch-down

redbrown.linePrev: And I miss your precious heart
stanza: Dried rose petals, redbrown circles
Framed your eyes and
Stained your knuckles
Dried rose petals, redbrown circles
Framed your eyes and
Stained your knuckles

resolve.linePrev: In the night, in the night, you may hear me call
resolve.line: Pa, stay your hand and steel your resolve
resolve.lineNext: Stay where you are, so long and tall
stanza: By the time you read this, I will be so far away
Daddy Longlegs, how in the world am I to be expected to stay?
In the night, in the night, you may hear me call
Pa, stay your hand and steel your resolve
Stay where you are, so long and tall

resolve.linePrev: May those who find you find remorse
resolve.line: A change of course, a strange discord resolved
stanza: Drawn in chalk across the floor
You made it yours
Your private door to my room
May those who find you find remorse
A change of course, a strange discord resolved

road's.linePrev: Just get us out, dead or alive
road's.line: The road's too long to mention--
stanza: And I did not mean to shout. Just drive
Just get us out, dead or alive
The road's too long to mention--
Lord, it's something to see!--
Laid down by the
Good Intentions Paving Company
All the way to the thing
We've been playing at, darling
I can see that you're wearing
Your staying-hat, darling

road's.linePrev: Little darling thinks the road is soft and lovely
road's.line: I'm thankful that old road's a friend of mine
stanza: Aw, Mother thinks the road is long and lonely
Little brother thinks the road is straight and fine
Little darling thinks the road is soft and lovely
I'm thankful that old road's a friend of mine
I believe, regardless
I believe in everyone

rharian.linePrev: A cave, a grave, a day: arise, ascend
rharian.line: (Areion, Rharian, go free and graze. Amen.)
rharian.lineNext: A shore, a tide, unmoored–a sight, abroad:
stanza: Joy! Again, around–a pause, a sound–a song:
A way a lone a last a loved a long
A cave, a grave, a day: arise, ascend
(Areion, Rharian, go free and graze. Amen.)

The snow falls above me

repair.linePrev: So we all raise a standard
repair.line: To which the wise and honest soul may repair
stanza: So we all raise a standard
To which the wise and honest soul may repair
To which a hunter
A hundred years from now, may look and despair
And see with wonder
The tributes we have left to rust in the park
Swearing that our hair stood on end
To see John Purroy Mitchel depart
For the Western Front
Where work might count
All exeunt! All go out!
Await the hunter, to decipher the stone
(and what lies under, now)
The city is gone

partner.line: A hundred years from now, may look and despair

repair.line: Repair is the dream of the broken thing
repair.lineNext: Like a message broadcast on an overpass
stanza: Repair is the dream of the broken thing
Like a message broadcast on an overpass
All my favorite singers couldn't sing
All my favorite singers couldn't sing

rufous.linePrev: Anecdotes cannot say what Time may do
rufous.lineNext: When our men are all asleep:
stanza: I kid with Rufous Nightjar
When our men are all asleep:
“It ain’t about how rare you are
But how hard you are to see
Take, you and me—"

raid.linePrev: Must every debt now be repaid
raid.lineNext: While we sing to the garden, and we sing to the stars
stanza: But, inasmuch as that light is loaned
And, insofar as we’ve borrowed bones
Must every debt now be repaid
In star-spotted, sickle-winged night raids
While we sing to the garden, and we sing to the stars
And we sing in the meantime
Wherever you are?

partner.line: Must every debt now be repaid

Lighter borne than the pain of death?
I ain't saying that I loved you first
But I loved you best

rooftop.linePrev: I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
rooftop.lineNext: I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
stanza: Welcome to hell
I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
Welcome to rooftops
I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
Welcome to the starlight room
I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
Welcome to the underworld
And now, for tonight’s main event
The heavyweight
Close swaddled in the cloying folds of fat
For heavyweight
Squats freak fearful on his stool
And through toad lids he squints his vision of the world
The gray parade of waiters and trade is retinue of course
Seconds thirds and fourths
Clean tick-like to his skin
The bell sounds in the corner of the ring a fresh contender for his crown
Puts out his pale hand to shake before the bout
Champion blinks his sticky tongue swallowing greed
With cuprous speed snakes out

rooftop.line: And you're wrecking all the rooftops
stanza: My baby is a tornado
In the endless Oklahoma sky
Spinning devastation
And singing me a lullaby
And you're wrecking all the rooftops
When April turns to May
It wouldn't make a difference
If I could or couldn't stay
I fall back into love
And look up and then you're gone
But I still feel you storming in my bones

rudy.linePrev: Like some forgotten dream that we've both seen
rudy.line: Someday I'll go and call up Rudy
rudy.lineNext: We worked together at the factory
stanza: Me and Loretta, we don't talk much more
She sits and stares through the backdoor screen
And all the news just repeats itself
Like some forgotten dream that we've both seen
Someday I'll go and call up Rudy
We worked together at the factory
But what could I say if he asks "What's new?"
"Nothing, what's with you? Nothing much to do"

remorse.lineNext: A change of course, a strange discord resolved
stanza: Drawn in chalk across the floor
You made it yours
Your private door to my room
May those who find you find remorse
A change of course, a strange discord resolved

partner.line: Drawn in chalk across the floor

rode.linePrev: And I dreamed I was a cowboy
rode.line: And I rode across a border
rode.lineNext: If you love me, say I love you
stanza: So I drank myself some whisky
And I dreamed I was a cowboy
And I rode across a border

rode.line: And I rode alongside you then
rode.lineNext: And I rode alongside till you lost me there in the open road
stanza: And I ride alongside
And I rode alongside you then
And I rode alongside till you lost me there in the open road
And I rode alongside till the honey spread itself so thin
For me to break your bread, for me to take your word
I had to steal it

rode.linePrev: And the buckeye sun was a-shinin'
rode.line: I rode my bike down Alum Creek Drive
rode.lineNext: When I was a boy in Ohio
stanza: Creek was running by the road
And the buckeye sun was a-shinin'
I rode my bike down Alum Creek Drive
When I was a boy in Ohio
The English teacher he didn't care
He challenged us to checkers
And once in a while we'd swap a joke
When I was a boy in Ohio

rode.linePrev: And I rode alongside till you lost me there in the open road
rode.line: And I rode alongside till the honey spread itself so thin
rode.lineNext: For me to break your bread, for me to take your word
stanza: And I ride alongside
And I rode alongside you then
And I rode alongside till you lost me there in the open road
And I rode alongside till the honey spread itself so thin
For me to break your bread, for me to take your word
I had to steal it

rode.linePrev: And I rode alongside you then
rode.line: And I rode alongside till you lost me there in the open road
rode.lineNext: And I rode alongside till the honey spread itself so thin
stanza: And I ride alongside
And I rode alongside you then
And I rode alongside till you lost me there in the open road
And I rode alongside till the honey spread itself so thin
For me to break your bread, for me to take your word
I had to steal it
Once we rode together
In a metal firecracker
You told me I was your queen
You told me I was your biker
You told me I was your everything

rode.linePrev: I used to be so kind
rode.line: Never rode my bike down to the sea
rode.lineNext: Never finished that letter I was writing
stanza: Never rode my bike down to the sea
Never finished that letter I was writing
Never got up and said anything
Worthy, for he, for my

rode.linePrev: Well, you wore out your welcome with random precision
rode.lineNext: Come on, you raver, you seer of visions
stanza: Well, you wore out your welcome with random precision
Rode on the steel breeze
Come on, you raver, you seer of visions
Come on, you painter, you piper, you prisoner, and shine

rode.linePrev: They say he jumped ten thousand trains
rode.line: Rode a million miles for free
stanza: 2 ContributorsLast of the Hobo Kings LyricsSteam Train Maury died last night
His wife Wanda by his side
He caught the Westbound out of here
Hopped the high irons to the by and by
They say he jumped ten thousand trains
Rode a million miles for free
Helped out at VA hospitals
And penitentiaries
Dandy Dave, Rusty Nails and Sweet Lady Sugar Cane
Dead Eye Kate and the Baloney Kid raise their cups tonight in Steam Train's name
Senators, congressmen, puppets on a string
Among the windswept vagabonds Steam Train was the king
The last of the hobo kings
The last of the hobo kings

ryan.linePrev: Thinking he knew where I was going
ryan.lineNext: I think he thinks it makes me weaker, but it only ever makes me strong
stanza: Thinking he knew where I was going
He put Ryan Adams on
I think he thinks it makes me weaker, but it only ever makes me strong

revoke.line: And I have seen lives revoked
revoke.lineNext: And I looked at my life and I choked
stanza: I have seen men provoked
And I have seen lives revoked
And I looked at my life and I choked
From there no more ever I spoke

Got lost in the fray, I gave all I had for a time
Then by some strange design, I got a case of the empties
The ruler of my world, a lost forgotten pearl
When fire leaves a girl
Too burned to try my life
Living on the fault line
And at night

rely.line: You can rely on me honey
rely.lineNext: You can combine anything you want
stanza: Jesus, don't cry
You can rely on me honey
You can combine anything you want
I'll be around
You were right about the stars
Each one is a setting sun
Oh, I've got reservations
About so many things
But not about you
I've reservations
About so many things
But not about you

reservation.linePrev: None of this is real enough to take me from you
stanza: Oh, I've got reservations
About so many things
But not about you
